trajectory_summary: The call never built energy because it never had a foundation. Carter opened with a fabricated premise ('estimate already built'), skipped the entire verify section, and spent the call chasing a scheduling slot that John was never fully committed to. When John's computer-failure excuse surfaced, Carter had no rapport, no bill anchor, and no curiosity created to hold John in the conversation. The call ended with a soft callback promise — no locked time, no verbal handshake — which is functionally a no-booking.
outcome_dispute: The v1 outcome tag is unknown, but the call should be tagged as no_appointment / soft_callback_only. Carter did not secure a booked appointment. The closest thing to a commitment is John saying 'just text me' at 02:46, with no specific time locked. Per analysis_directives.soft_outcome_distinction, this is not a sit-eligible outcome.

primary_coaching_focus.quote: And then a couple of things I need to check first, though. So there's a huge tax incentive. You You probably heard that. You just need to pay income tax and have a credit score above six fifty. Is that the case for you?
primary_coaching_focus.why: Carter went from a fabricated opener directly to the credit qualifier with zero verify beats completed. No bill anchor, no co-owner check, no interest-in-solar depth, no roof age, no prior designs. This is not a pacing choice — it is a structural omission that left Carter with no emotional leverage when John started pulling away at 01:52. Every other gap on this call (soft callback, no handshake, missed enthusiasm signal) is downstream of this one. Fix the verify discipline first and the rest of the call has a foundation to stand on.

key_moments.quote: I'm just calling because we got that solar estimate built for your home. Just haven't been able to get it out to you yet. So I just need to find a good time for our specialist to bring that over.
key_moments.stewart_read: Carter opens with a fabricated premise — an estimate that was 'already built' but 'hasn't been sent yet' — rather than either the Tesla intro or the standard form-fill intro. This is not a strategic choice; there is no later move it sets up. It is a reactive improvisation that skips legitimacy framing entirely and manufactures false urgency. The customer never pushes back on the premise, but he also never engages with it — he moves straight to scheduling logistics.

key_moments.quote: And then a couple of things I need to check first, though. So there's a huge tax incentive. You You probably heard that. You just need to pay income tax and have a credit score above six fifty. Is that the case for you?
key_moments.stewart_read: Carter jumps from the fabricated opener directly to the credit qualifier, skipping every verify beat: no address confirmation, no title/co-owners, no interest_in_solar, no utility company, no average bill, no roof type, no prior designs. The call_shape requires verify before qualifiers. Carter also states the credit threshold as 650, not 670 — a schema drift flagged in qualifiers.credit_qualifier.drift_observed. The verify section is not a formality; it is where bill pain is anchored and spouse/co-owner status is captured. Skipping it means Carter has no emotional leverage and no knowledge of whether a co-decision-maker exists.

key_moments.quote: Which, we have two houses. Which house did you do it for? The 26, 170 or the 26474?
key_moments.stewart_read: John volunteers that he owns two properties. This is a material lead-expansion signal: a second property is a second potential solar install, a second roofing opportunity, and a second sit. Carter's only response is to confirm which address the 'estimate' was built for — he does not acknowledge the second property, does not ask anything about it, and moves on. The signal is walked past entirely.

key_moments.quote: I'm I'm real busy right now. Oh, I see it. I can't do it on Tuesday. Sorry. I have an appointment then.
key_moments.stewart_read: John's 'real busy' signal arrives with zero rapport buffer. Carter had no bill pain on the table, no curiosity created, no emotional alignment built. When scheduling friction hit, there was nothing holding John in the conversation. This is the downstream consequence of skipping the verify section — the call had no anchor to return to when John started pulling away.
